Proposition 2.16. Characterization of Nonnegative Elements of \(L^{p}[a, b]\).

An element \(\phi \in L^{p}[a, b]\) is non-negative iff there exists a Cauchy sequence \(\{ c_{k}\}\) representing \(\phi\) such that \(c_{k}^{-}:=\min\{ c_{k}(x), 0\}\) satisfies \(\Vert c_{k}^{-} \Vert_{L^{p}}\to 0\) as \(k\to \infty\text{.}\)
As a consequence, if \(\phi \in L^{p}[a, b]\) is non-negative, then \(\int_{[a, b]} \phi \ge 0\text{.}\) 2 

Proof.

in-context
